videos.title: I Built the Eridian Clock from Project Hail Mary
videos.summary: To celebrate Project Hail Mary, I built two Eridian-inspired clocks based on the version described in the book: a compact digital wristwatch and a larger analog clock. This video is an overview of the design, custom 3D-printed parts, electronics, and assembly process behind both builds.

videos.title: How to send binary data using LASERS!!! (DIY Arduino project)
videos.summary: In this video, we go over the process of setting up a sender and receiver hardware and software, so that we can send data using lasers. You can download the Arduino code used in this project here: https://alexinflatland.com/how-to-send-binary-data-using-lasers/ And here’s a list of the components used and where you...

videos.title: Gaming on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 3 - Why I'm NEVER going back to a non folding phone!
videos.summary: I've been gaming and developing games on this phone for 2 months now. These are my thoughts on it from a gamer's perspective. Spoiler alert, I'm really enjoying it! P.S. How crazy would it be if someone actually bought this phone through my affiliate link :) https://amzn.to/3nzN6tA

videos.title: How to make a SCREAMING PUMPKIN for Halloween to scare your unsuspecting neighbors!
videos.summary: A simple DIY project using an Arduino and an Adafruit Music Shield that will have you make a screaming pumpkin for Halloween, and hopefully get a few jump scares out of your unsuspecting neighbors. You can find the Arduino code here: https://alexinflatland.com/how-to-make-a-screaming-pumpkin-for-halloween-to-scare-...

videos.title: How to replace the battery on a Fitbit Alta HR
videos.summary: In this video I show you how to replace the battery on a Fitbit Alta HR. You can purchase a replacement battery and tools required here: https://amzn.to/3Gd4L2x I also have a video on the Fitbit Alta: https://youtu.be/5ECs_vvbSe0

videos.title: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 Multiple Monitor Setup Problem and Solution (Game Dev Perspective)
videos.summary: In this video, we're going to take a look at what's the problem with setting up multiple monitors in Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and how we can solve that problem. I also go deeper and explain what I believe the problem is from a game developer's point of view, and what I think the developers of the simulator...

videos.title: Running an Arduino on a breadboard with NO EXTERNAL COMPONENTS / How to FLASH an Arduino BOOTLOADER
videos.summary: In this video, we discuss how to run an Arduino on a breadboard with no external components, and how to flash an Arduino bootloader onto an ATmega chip. Bootloader files per IDE version: 1.6.x+ - https://alexinflatland.com/downloads/arduino_bootloaders/breadboard-1-6-x.zip 1.5.x -...

videos.title: How to run an Arduino ATmega328P on a Breadboard and How to Program it using a USB to Serial Adapter
videos.summary: In this video we discuss how to run an Arduino on a breadboard, and how to program it using a USB to Serial Adapter. How to program an Arduino on a breadboard using a USB to Serial Adapter: 23:40 How to run an Arduino on a breadboard with NO EXTERNAL COMPONENTS: https://youtu.be/fzZ2TR_mwgs

videos.title: How to Fix ERROR CODE 0x97 on EPSON Printer / How To Clean EPSON Printer Head
videos.summary: In this video I explore 3 different approaches to fixing EPSON Error Code 0x97 that's primarily caused by a clogged printer head. One of the approaches involves manually cleaning your EPSON printer head.

videos.title: How to replace the battery on a Fitbit Alta
videos.summary: In this video I show you how to replace the battery on a Fitbit Alta. I also have a video on the Fitbit Alta HR: https://youtu.be/aE_8u2wtIOI You can purchase a replacement battery and tools required here: https://amzn.to/3jws6T1

videos.title: How to DISABLE the data WRITE PROTECTION on an EEPROM chip (using an Arduino or a programmer)
videos.summary: In this video I'll walk you to how the data/write protection works in EEPROM chips, and how you can disable it using an Arduino DIY programmer or a commercial programmer. Timestamps: 0:00 - Data protection problem 3:52 - Data protection algorithm 6:53 - ATmega port registers 9:57 - Disabling write protection using...

videos.title: How to replace the battery on a Fitbit Blaze (fixes overheating issue)
videos.summary: In this video I show you how to replace the battery on a Fitbit Blaze. This also fixed an issue I had where my Fitbit would overheat while charging. You can purchase a replacement battery here: Battery with tools: https://amzn.to/3GgkFsP Battery without tools: https://amzn.to/3CloxGA

videos.title: BarTop Arcade Cabinet running MAME32 - The Making Of
videos.summary: Here's my take on making myself a bar-top arcade cabinet. All it's missing is the final housing :) You can find more details about the build on my blog, located here: http://alexinflatland.com/bartop-arcade-cabinet-running-mame32-convering-analog-joysticks-to-digital-d-pads/

videos.title: M&M Sorting using OpenCV and Arduino - Part 2
videos.summary: This is part 2 of my M&M sorter project. Here you can see how it actually functions. For a more detailed explanation on how it's built, check out part 1 here: http://youtu.be/wAQINPSFfOg For more details and the code used to run this thing, check out my blog entry here: http://alexinflatland.com/mm-sorter-using-...

videos.title: M&M Sorting using OpenCV and Arduino - Part 1
videos.summary: This is an M&M sorter that I designed and built using some spare parts from an old printer, an Arduino, a webcam and OpenCV. This particular video is part 1 and shows how the project is built and how its sub-parts function. In part 2 you can actually see it running. You can find part 2 here:...

videos.title: Tracking a ball and rotating camera with OpenCV and Arduino
videos.summary: This is a little project I built using OpenCV and an Arduino Uno. OpenCV tracks a ball, and the Arduino controls a servo motor to rotate the camera in case the ball goes off-screen. That way you get a tracking range of well over 180 degrees. You can find the code used in this project (both the OpenCV and the Arduino...
